source_text: The keys found in certificates MUST also be of appropriate type for the signature algorithms they are used with.
rule_statement: Certificate keys MUST be of appropriate type for the signature algorithms they are used with (e.g., RSA key for RSA-PSS signatures).
applies_when: Endpoint uses a certificate key with a signature algorithm
expected_value: key type matches signature algorithm requirements
checkability_rationale: Can verify key type matches the algorithm used in CertificateVerify or certificate chain.

source_text: Clients which desire the server to authenticate itself via a certificate MUST send the "signature_algorithms" extension.
rule_statement: Clients that want server certificate authentication MUST send the signature_algorithms extension in ClientHello.
checkability_rationale: Can verify extension is included when certificate auth is expected.

source_text: If a server is authenticating via a certificate and the client has not sent a "signature_algorithms" extension, then the server MUST abort the handshake with a "missing_extension" alert (see Section 9.2).
rule_statement: If the server authenticates via certificate and the client did not send signature_algorithms, the server MUST abort with a missing_extension alert.
applies_when: Server wants to authenticate via certificate but ClientHello lacks signature_algorithms
checkability_rationale: Can verify server checks for extension presence and aborts with correct alert.

source_text: The length of the Salt MUST be equal to the length of the output of the digest algorithm.
rule_statement: For RSASSA-PSS RSAE algorithms, the salt length MUST equal the output length of the digest algorithm.
applies_when: Endpoint generates or verifies an RSASSA-PSS RSAE signature
checkability_rationale: Can verify PSS parameters set salt length equal to hash output length.

source_text: If the public key is carried in an X.509 certificate, it MUST use the rsaEncryption OID [RFC5280].
rule_statement: For RSASSA-PSS RSAE algorithms, the public key in X.509 certificate MUST use the rsaEncryption OID.
applies_when: Certificate uses RSASSA-PSS RSAE algorithm with X.509 public key
checkability_rationale: Can verify OID in certificate public key info.

source_text: The length of the Salt MUST be equal to the length of the digest algorithm.
rule_statement: For RSASSA-PSS PSS algorithms, the salt length MUST equal the output length of the digest algorithm.
applies_when: Endpoint generates or verifies an RSASSA-PSS PSS signature
checkability_rationale: Can verify PSS parameters set salt length equal to hash output length.

source_text: If the public key is carried in an X.509 certificate, it MUST use the RSASSA-PSS OID [RFC5756].
rule_statement: For RSASSA-PSS PSS algorithms, the public key in X.509 certificate MUST use the RSASSA-PSS OID.
applies_when: Certificate uses RSASSA-PSS PSS algorithm with X.509 public key

source_text: When used in certificate signatures, the algorithm parameters MUST be DER encoded.
rule_statement: When RSASSA-PSS PSS is used in certificate signatures, the algorithm parameters MUST be DER encoded.
applies_when: RSASSA-PSS PSS algorithm is used in certificate signatures
checkability_rationale: Can verify DER encoding of algorithm parameters.

source_text: If the corresponding public key's parameters are present, then the parameters in the signature MUST be identical to those in the public key.
rule_statement: If the public key has RSASSA-PSS parameters, the signature parameters MUST be identical to the public key parameters.
applies_when: RSASSA-PSS PSS public key has parameters present
checkability_rationale: Direct comparison of signature and public key parameters.

source_text: Clients offering these values MUST list them as the lowest priority (listed after all other algorithms in SignatureSchemeList).
rule_statement: Clients offering legacy SHA-1 signature algorithms MUST list them as the lowest priority (last in SignatureSchemeList).
applies_when: Client includes rsa_pkcs1_sha1 or ecdsa_sha1 in signature_algorithms
expected_value: SHA-1 schemes listed after all other algorithms
checkability_rationale: Can verify ordering of algorithm list in the extension.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST with clear ordering requirement.

source_text: TLS 1.3 servers MUST NOT offer a SHA-1 signed certificate unless no valid certificate chain can be produced without it (see Section 4.4.2.2).
rule_statement: TLS 1.3 servers MUST NOT offer a SHA-1 signed certificate unless no valid certificate chain can be produced without it.
applies_when: Server selects a certificate chain for TLS 1.3
expected_value: no SHA-1 signed certificates unless absolutely necessary
checkability_rationale: Can verify certificate selection avoids SHA-1, but the 'unless no valid chain' exception requires context.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST NOT with stated exception.

source_text: TLS 1.3 implementations willing to negotiate TLS 1.2 MUST behave in accordance with the requirements of [RFC5246] when negotiating that version.
rule_statement: TLS 1.3 implementations that negotiate TLS 1.2 MUST behave per RFC 5246 requirements for that version.
checkability_rationale: Requires full RFC 5246 conformance check — very broad scope.

source_text: They MUST NOT be offered or negotiated by any implementation. In particular, MD5 [SLOTH], SHA-224, and DSA MUST NOT be used.
rule_statement: Deprecated legacy hash/signature pairs (MD5, SHA-224, DSA) MUST NOT be offered or negotiated by any implementation.
applies_when: Any TLS 1.3 implementation constructs or processes signature algorithm lists
expected_value: no MD5, SHA-224, or DSA algorithms
checkability_rationale: Can verify that MD5, SHA-224, and DSA code points are not included.

source_text: If TLS 1.2 is negotiated, implementations MUST be prepared to accept a signature that uses any curve that they advertised in the "supported_groups" extension.
rule_statement: When TLS 1.2 is negotiated, implementations MUST accept ECDSA signatures using any curve they advertised in supported_groups.
applies_when: TLS 1.2 is negotiated and ECDSA signatures are used
expected_value: accept any advertised curve for ECDSA
checkability_rationale: Requires testing signature verification with different curves.

source_text: Implementations that advertise support for RSASSA-PSS (which is mandatory in TLS 1.3) MUST be prepared to accept a signature using that scheme even when TLS 1.2 is negotiated.
rule_statement: Implementations advertising RSASSA-PSS support MUST accept PSS signatures even when TLS 1.2 is negotiated.
applies_when: TLS 1.2 is negotiated and implementation advertises RSASSA-PSS
checkability_rationale: Requires testing PSS signature verification under TLS 1.2 code path.
